title: Tithymalus × angustatus
taxonomy: Tithymalus × angustatus is a hybrid species within the Euphorbiaceae family. This classification suggests that it belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. The genus Tithymalus is a part of this family and is typically found in the Northern Hemisphere, particularly in Europe and Asia. Tithymalus × angustatus is likely classified as a hybrid between two different species within the Tithymalus genus. More specific classification information may require further research into the specific species that Tithymalus × angustatus is derived from.

intro: Tithymalus × angustatus, also known as Euphorbia × angustata, is a hybrid plant species within the spurge family (Euphorbiaceae), characterized by its unique, succulent stems and leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and wound healing, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tithymalus × angustatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and diterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, this plant is often used to prepare tonics, salves, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies such as teas, tinctures, and poultices.
